LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF USED DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT. THE MARTIN DECLARATION DOES NOT NEGATE PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO PROVE THE CASE. PACO WAS NOT EXPLORED IN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION. THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE STORES IN WASHINGTON DOES NOT NEGATE PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO PROVE THE PRODUCT WAS PURCHASED AT A STORE OTHER THAN DEFENDANT'S STORE. THERE ALSO WOULD BE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE BAGS PURCHASED BY PLAINTIFF WERE DEFENDANT'S BAGS. MR. COOK IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J.
